text: The new Teams onboarding made setup way faster. We formed our first team in minutes.
Clearer steps in the Teams checklist. Our admins finally completed provisioning on day one.
Couldn’t create or sync teams for hours today. We lost a morning of work.
Outage hit our APAC offices. Status page was slow to update.
Core is good but we need multi-user roles. Considering upgrading to Teams if pricing is fair.
SSO + SCIM are stable so far. Would like more granular audit logs.
Shared teams reduced email churn. Worth the extra cost for our small team.
We saw a price increase during trial. At this price, we’ll stay on Core.
Our departments adopted Teams rooms weekly. Engagement is up.
Still cautious after the outage. Boss asked to pause rollout to the rest of APAC.
Onboarding hints appear for Teams features I can’t use. Slightly confusing.
Good DLP controls. Need clearer documentation on cross-region data residency.
We actually finished the setup wizard this time. Kudos.
Price is a stretch for our Manila office. If you kept old pricing we’d upgrade.
Training cost went down because the new Teams checklist is simple.
Got mixed messages about Teams trials and prices. Sales couldn’t clarify.
Speed is acceptable. No issues since March 12 in our region.
Leadership still references the March outage as a reason to wait on Teams.
Our CSM was proactive after the outage. That built trust.
We upgraded from Core. Shared spaces and roles are paying off already.
